task_completion.description: Open the train derailment manifest to identify the UN numbers for the involved tank cars.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of 'train_derailment_manifest.txt', showing tank car IDs (TC-101, TC-102, etc.) and their corresponding UN numbers.

task_completion.description: Search for the identified chemicals in CAMEO Chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals interface (web browser or app) showing search results or chemical profiles for UN 1050 (Hydrogen Chloride) and UN 1005 (Ammonia).

task_completion.description: Assess the chemical reaction between TC-101 and TC-102 using CAMEO Chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals reactivity worksheet or a reaction prediction page showing the interaction between Hydrogen Chloride and Ammonia, indicating an exothermic reaction or toxic hazard.

task_completion.description: Determine the isolation distances for the leaking chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: CAMEO Chemicals interface displaying the Emergency Response Guidebook (ERG) data, specifically the initial isolation and protective action distances for UN 1050 and/or UN 1005.

task_completion.description: Save a comprehensive emergency response assessment to the specified output file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing a document saved as 'train_derailment_assessment.txt' in the Documents folder, containing the identified chemicals, the reaction assessment (ammonium chloride/white cloud), and isolation distances.

integrity.description: The agent actively used the CAMEO Chemicals interface rather than relying solely on internal knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual CAMEO Chemicals graphical user interface being navigated, with search queries or chemical data sheets visible.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to write the assessment rather than blindly echoing text via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad) or an interactive terminal editor (like nano, vim) is visibly open and being used to draft 'train_derailment_assessment.txt'.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the reaction data from the tool rather than hallucinating generic responses.
integrity.visual_evidence: The reaction details in the final assessment should align with the specific reactivity hazards shown in the CAMEO Chemicals screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: TC-101 contains UN 1050 (Hydrogen Chloride, anhydrous) and TC-102 contains UN 1005 (Ammonia, anhydrous). TC-103 is UN 2209 (Formaldehyde solutions) and TC-104 is UN 1791 (Hypochlorite solutions). The key reaction between TC-101 and TC-102 is HCl(g) + NH3(g) -> NH4Cl(s), which produces a dense white aerosol cloud of ammonium chloride. This reaction is highly exothermic and creates an extreme inhalation hazard. The final assessment must be saved to ~/Documents/train_derailment_assessment.txt.
